Entry Thesis: Theta decay experiment — compare decay across two expiry dates. Both strikes 13-15% OTM at entry (AVAV at $278)
Market Context: Crisis Volatility — Iran conflict broke over weekend, hammered markets
Risk Management Plan: Credit received: $4,280. 100% stop at $8,560 debit ($21.40/spread). No layered alerts set (pre-enhancement). Stop fired and saved $8-10k.
Exit Reasoning: Stop fired within 90 minutes as AVAV dropped sharply on Iran news. AVAV subsequently fell to $204 (26% decline). Stop saved estimated $8,000-$10,000 in further losses
Lessons Learned: THE STOP SAVED $8,000-$10,000. Market orders gave away ~30% of premium at entry. Limit orders now non-negotiable. Earnings check missed (AVAV earnings Mar 10). Experimental trades must be sized at 50% or less of standard positions
Mistakes: Market order execution (filled $5.20-$5.50 vs ~$7 mark). No earnings check (AVAV earnings 8 days away). No layered alerts. Position 4.2x standard $1,000 CSP target
Tags: Diagonal, AVAV, Experimental, Stop-Loss, Crisis, Loss

Market Context: Market volatility continuing from prior week
Risk Management Plan: Premium at risk: $1,620. 50% profit target ($810). 50% loss alert ($810). 100% stop ($1,620).
Exit Reasoning: Stock dropped far ITM causing first margin call. Stop on premium was not triggered because the drop in underlying did not cause premium to rise enough to hit stop level. Had to buy to close at a loss to meet margin requirements.
Lessons Learned: CRITICAL LESSON: Premium-based stops alone are insufficient for deep ITM moves. Underlying stock price can breach strike significantly before put premium triggers a 100% stop (due to delta, time value remaining). Need dual stop: (1) premium-based stop AND (2) stop on underlying stock price. Also need more margin headroom to avoid forced closes.
Mistakes: No stop on underlying price. Insufficient margin buffer. Stop framework gap exposed — premium stops don't fire fast enough on sharp underlying moves.

Entry Thesis: 0DTE SNDK bull put spread. NO NEWS CHECK — WD secondary offering at $545 created supply shock and price anchor. 15% ITM probability was meaningless with known catalyst.
Market Context: Western Digital dumped 5.8M shares via secondary offering at $545, well below $772 market price.
Risk Management Plan: NO STOP SET. Was not placed before entry due to timing (personal commitments). Stops must be set BEFORE or IMMEDIATELY AFTER entry — no exceptions.
Exit Reasoning: Saw -$6,000, then recovery to -$1,800 when spread moved back OTM. FROZE. Did not close on the bounce. Spread went back ITM. Cost of inaction: ~$5,100 (difference between bounce exit and final loss).
Lessons Learned: CRITICAL: (1) Stops are non-negotiable — set at entry or don't enter. (2) 0DTE needs news/event filter — no premium selling on names with active corporate events. (3) Bounce = exit — if position breaches stop and recovers, close immediately. (4) Consider avoiding Friday trading until automated stop protection exists. Two consecutive weeks of Friday losses. (5) Froze under pressure — log the feeling, use it to fuel automation.
Mistakes: No stop set (timing/commitments). No news check (WD secondary offering). Froze on bounce instead of closing. Competing priorities (partner/daughter waiting, no phone signal). Largest single trade loss to date.
Tags: Bull Put Spread, SNDK, 0DTE, Loss, No-stop, Freeze, Framework-failure
